annotations.description: Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata. They are not queryable and should be preserved when modifying objects.

extraEnvs.description: Allows set extra system environment variables for cloud-events-exporter container.

exclude.description: Array of filters to exclude events.

match.description: Array of filters to match events.

image.description:
Image to use for cloud-events-exporter deployment.
Defaults: ghcr.io/netcracker/qubership-kube-events-reader:main

install.description: Allow to disable create cloud-events-exporter during deploy.

labels.description: Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects. May match selectors of replication controllers and services.

name.description: Provide a name in place of cloud-events-exporter for labels.

namespaces.description: List of namespaces to monitor for events.

nodeSelector.description: Allows to define which Nodes the Pods are scheduled on.

port.description: Port to scrape cloud events metrics.

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClassName assigned to the Pods to prevent them from evicting.

createClusterRole.description: Allow creating ClusterRole. If set to false, ClusterRole must be created manually.

createClusterRoleBinding.description: Allow creating ClusterRoleBinding. If set to false, ClusterRoleBinding must be created manually.

resources.description: Define resources requests and limits for single Pods.

limits.description: Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/#meaning-of-cpu-and-memory

requests.description: Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required. If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified, otherwise to an implementation-defined value. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/#meaning-of-cpu-and-memory

securityContext.description: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes. The parameters are required if a Pod Security Policy is enabled for Kubernetes cluster and required if a Security Context Constraints is enabled for Openshift cluster.

runAsUser.description:
The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process.
Defaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified.

fsGroup.description: A special supplemental group that applies to all containers in a pod.

serviceAccount.description: Service account for cloud-events-exporter to use.

annotations.description: Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata.

install.description: Specifies whether a ServiceAccount should be created.

labels.description: Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize objects. May match selectors of replication controllers and services.

serviceMonitor.description: Service monitor for scraping Cloud events as metrics.

install.description: Specifies whether a ServiceMonitor should be created.

interval.description: Interval at which to scrape the metrics.

metricRelabelings.description: MetricRelabelings to apply to samples before ingestion.

relabelings.description: Relabelings to apply to the scrape request before scraping.

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout for scraping metrics. Note that scrapeTimeout must be less than the interval.

tolerations.description: Tolerations allow the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints.
